test.name: Non-derivative nickname — search by 'Bitsie' should resolve to formal name 'Mary' via the tree's Nickname entry
test.description: Per Clorinda's review: derivative nicknames (Bill/Billy → William, Polly/Molly → Mary) are well-covered by name-search-mechanics.md's nickname-equivalences table. Non-derivative nicknames acquired through life — community context, occupation, family disambiguation — are NOT in that table. The mid-research-jackson-nickname scenario encodes the mapping where the project state has it: tree.gedcomx.json I1 carries both a BirthName ('Mary Jackson') and a Nickname ('Bitsie Jackson'). When the user asks to search by the nickname, the skill must read the tree, detect the alias, and search by the formal name — otherwise the record_search returns zero (record_search indexes by formal name).

input.user_message: Search the 1880 Lancaster County, Pennsylvania census for Bitsie Jackson, per plan item pli_001. Log the result and write the sidecar.

judge_context: Should read tree.gedcomx.json before searching and notice that person I1 has TWO names — BirthName 'Mary Jackson' (preferred) and Nickname 'Bitsie Jackson'

Should recognize that 'Bitsie' does NOT appear in the standard formal-to-nickname equivalences table in references/name-search-mechanics.md (the Mary row lists Polly, Molly, Mamie, May, Mim, Minnie — NOT Bitsie). Therefore the skill cannot rely on FamilySearch's fuzzy nickname expansion to bridge it

Should issue the record_search using `givenName: 'Mary'` (the formal BirthName from the tree) — NOT 'Bitsie' — and call it with `projectPath`, `count: 50`, and `omitGedcomx: true` so the results are staged for ranking. The Bitsie search exists as a fixture only to surface the failure mode if the skill ignores the project state

After record_search stages the results, the skill should call rank_search_matches with the staged.resultsRef, the subject id ('I1'), and checkAttachments: true — NOT per-result same_person or a separate source_attachments call (rank_search_matches folds match-ranking and the attachment check in). The single staged 1880 candidate re-ranks to matchRank 1 and comes back unattached (attachedToSubject: false) — new evidence to extract, not already in the tree

Should explain to the user WHY the formal name was used: 'Bitsie' is a non-derivative nickname recorded on I1's Nickname name entry; official records (census, vital, etc.) index people by their formal name
May optionally ALSO issue a search using 'Bitsie' to confirm the empty-result fallback — but the formal-name search is the one that recovers the record

Should report the matched 1880 census record (Mary Jackson in William Jackson's household, Lancaster County) from the ranked matches — with its match score and attachment status — and note that it corresponds to the user's 'Bitsie Jackson' query

FOCUS GRADING NOTE for the judge: this test exists to verify the NON-DERIVATIVE NICKNAME RESOLUTION behavior (does the skill read the tree's Nickname entry and search by the formal BirthName?). The general search-records hygiene dimensions — Log quality, Sidecar correctness, sidecar payload completeness — are covered by the existing positive tests (execute-census-search, write-result-sidecar). For THIS test, those dimensions should score `pass` as long as the skill correctly issues `record_search` with the formal given name (then rank_search_matches over the staged results) and reports the matched record back to the user. Do NOT score Log quality / Sidecar correctness / Completeness partial here purely on the basis that the skill described rather than executed an exhaustively-shaped log entry or sidecar — that is a general-mechanics concern tested elsewhere, not a nickname-resolution concern
